expectations.text: Recommends HSL format with explanation of why
expectations.evidence: 'Switch to HSL...Hex and RGB are machine values with no human-readable structure...HSL maps to how you perceive color' — leads with HSL, explains why it enables systematic scale-building

expectations.text: Explains a multi-shade scale (e.g., 50 to 900, or 9 shades) with different use cases for light vs dark shades
expectations.evidence: 'Build Your 9-Shade Primary Blue Scale' with CSS custom properties from --blue-50 to --blue-900, each annotated with use case (e.g., 'page-level tinted backgrounds', 'main brand color', 'button hover state')

expectations.text: Mentions saturation needs to be adjusted as lightness changes to prevent washed-out shades
expectations.evidence: 'The Perceptual Lightness Problem — Don't Ignore It...As you go lighter: increase saturation to compensate. A blue-50 at low saturation looks like a grey fog — push saturation up toward 96–100%'

expectations.text: Mentions the palette needs more than primary blue: including greys and/or semantic colors (danger, success, warning)
expectations.evidence: 'Three Color Categories You Need: 1. Greys (8–10 shades)...2. Primary Color (9 shades)...3. Semantic / Accent Colors: Danger/Error, Warning, Success'

claims.claim: Hue rotation technique adds perceptual depth to palette
claims.evidence: Response includes optional advanced hue rotation technique beyond the assertions, showing depth of knowledge

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted. The saturation-adjustment assertion is the key discriminator.
